name: Fuentecantos and Chavaler - circular from Garray
name_original: Fuentecantos y Chavaler — circular desde Garray
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Garray are situated in the Duero River valley, characterized by its lush riverbanks and the confluence with the Tera River. The landscape features forested areas like Monte Valonsadero and open countryside, providing varied terrain for cyclists. Historical sites, including the Archaeological Site of Numancia and a sixteenth-century stone bridge, are accessible along many routes. The area offers a mix of flat river paths and gentle ascents, suitable for diverse touring cycling experiences. Best touring cycling routes around Garray The most popular touring cycling route is Monte Valonsadero Route 1, a 13.4 miles (21.6 km) trail that takes 1 hour 32 minutes to complete. This route leads through the forested Monte Valonsadero, offering tranquil river views. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Fuentecantos and Chavaler - circular from Garray, an easy 17.9 miles (28.8 km) path. This route explores the countryside around Garray, passing through rural villages and open landscapes. Local touring cyclists also love the Cemetery of Pedrajas loop from The Caddie, a 22.3 miles (36.0 km) trail leading through varied rural scenery, often completed in about 2 hours 56 minutes. Touring cycling around Garray is defined by the Duero River valley, forested sections of Monte Valonsadero, and historical landmarks like the Numancia archaeological site. The network offers options for easy rides along riverbanks, moderate routes through open countryside, and more challenging paths with greater elevation gain. The routes in Garray are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 70 reviews. More than 800 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Garray's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Garray?
A: There are over 100 touring cycling routes around Garray, offering a wide variety of experiences. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from nearly 80 reviews.

Q: What types of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Garray?
A: Garray's touring cycling routes offer diverse terrain. You'll find lush river paths along the Duero and Tera Rivers, forested sections through areas like Monte Valonsadero, and open countryside. The area features a mix of flat sections and gentle ascents, catering to various fitness levels.

Q: Are there any easy or family-friendly touring cycling routes in Garray?
A: Yes, Garray offers several easy touring cycling routes suitable for families and beginners. An excellent option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1042251034/monte-valonsadero-route-1" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte Valonsadero Route 1&lt;/a&gt;, which is 13.4 miles (21.6 km) long and takes you through tranquil forested areas with river views. Another easy choice is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1036861878/fuentecantos-and-chavaler-circular-from-garray" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuentecantos and Chavaler - circular from Garray&lt;/a&gt;, an 17.9-mile (28.8 km) path exploring rural villages and open landscapes.

Q: What historical sites or landmarks can I see while cycling around Garray?
A: Garray is rich in history. You can cycle past the significant &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4000052" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Archaeological Site of Numancia&lt;/a&gt;, an ancient Celtiberian city. The sixteenth-century stone bridge over the Duero River is another notable landmark. Further afield, but accessible by bike, are the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3936758" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita de los Santos Mártires&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3936758" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Iglesia de San Juan Bautista&lt;/a&gt; in the town center.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in the Garray area?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes around Garray are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1036861878/fuentecantos-and-chavaler-circular-from-garray" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuentecantos and Chavaler - circular from Garray&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 17.9-mile loop. For a more challenging option, consider the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1041627839/estepa-de-san-juan-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Estepa de San Juan — circular&lt;/a&gt; route, which covers over 38 miles (62.5 km).

Q: What are the best times of year for touring cycling in Garray?
A: The spring and autumn months are generally ideal for touring cycling in Garray. During these seasons, the weather is typically mild, and the natural landscapes, especially along the Duero River valley, are vibrant with flora. Summers can be warm, while winters are cooler, so plan accordingly.

Q: Are there any scenic viewpoints along the cycling routes?
A: Absolutely. The region offers stunning natural landscapes and river views. The Cerro de la Muela provides elevated perspectives, and the Mirón area is known for its beautiful vistas. You can also find specific viewpoints like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3342444" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View from Cerro de Santa Ana&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3943793" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de Las Cabezas Geodetic Point&lt;/a&gt;, which offer panoramic views of the surrounding area.

Q: Where can I find parking or access points for cycling routes in Garray?
A: Garray itself serves as an excellent starting point for many touring cycling routes, with various access points within the town. The path connecting Garray to Soria along the Duero River is particularly noted as a convenient and scenic option. Parking is generally available within Garray, especially near the town center or historical sites.

Q: Are there any challenging touring cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, Garray offers several difficult touring cycling routes for those seeking a challenge. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1041627839/estepa-de-san-juan-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Estepa de San Juan — circular&lt;/a&gt; route is a demanding 38.9-mile (62.5 km) ride with significant elevation gain. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10464379" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Garray – Almarza Village Center loop from Soria&lt;/a&gt;, covering 35.8 miles (57.7 km) with over 1,300 feet (400 meters) of ascent.

Q: What natural features define the cycling experience in Garray?
A: The touring cycling experience in Garray is largely defined by the picturesque Duero River valley, with the Tera River also contributing to lush riverbanks. You'll encounter rich flora, including poplars, ash trees, and willows, especially near the confluence of the rivers, known as 'La Junta de los Ríos.' Forested areas like Monte Valonsadero also provide tranquil natural settings for rides.

Q: Can I find waterfalls or other natural monuments along the routes?
A: While the region is known for its rivers and forests, you can find natural monuments nearby. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2999663" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cascada de la Toba&lt;/a&gt; is a notable waterfall in the vicinity. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4077489" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Golmayo River&lt;/a&gt; is another natural highlight worth exploring.
tips: A friendly town with a cute Romanesque church and a restored wash house with an old fountain.
A quiet town with a beautiful church with a neoclassical portal and that is next to the Tera river.
A small bridge that crosses the Pedrajas River.
Here the Duero and Pedrajas rivers meet and it is perfect to stop for lunch at their picnic area or sit quietly listening to the murmur of the river.

waypoints.name: Paso de las Carretas (Río Pedrajas Crossing)
Confluence of the Duero and Pedrajas Rivers
